title: Metro Spare-Parts Safety-Stock Placement
strategies: trivial: quote S_i=0 everywhere (decoupled; hold safety stock at every node for its own lead time) -> exactly the evaluator baseline -> ~0.1

greedy: push each node's outbound service to the max (tau=0 internally), dumping all accumulated lead time onto the s_max-capped stations, which carry the priciest stock -> feasible but below baseline
strong: Graves-Willems dynamic program over the tree -> exact optimal service-time placement, balancing risk pooling vs downstream holding cost and station caps -> ~0.24-0.43, headroom below 1.0
invalid: quote S_i=500 everywhere -> net replenishment time goes negative / station caps blown -> infeasible -> 0
